OS.question: What is an Operating System?
OS.short_answer: An interface between hardware and user that manages resources.

OS.question: What is a Process?
OS.short_answer: A program in execution.

OS.question: What is a Thread?
OS.short_answer: A lightweight process; basic unit of CPU utilization.

OS.question: Difference between Process and Thread?
OS.short_answer: Process has its own memory; threads share memory of the parent process.

OS.question: What is Deadlock?
OS.short_answer: A situation where processes are stuck waiting for each other's resources.

OS.question: What are the four conditions for Deadlock?
OS.short_answer: Mutual Exclusion, Hold and Wait, No Preemption, Circular Wait.

OS.question: What is Virtual Memory?
OS.short_answer: Memory management technique that creates an illusion of larger RAM.

OS.question: What is Paging?
OS.short_answer: Storage mechanism used to retrieve processes from secondary storage into main memory in pages.

OS.question: What is Segmentation?
OS.short_answer: Memory management where memory is divided into variable-sized segments.

OS.question: What is Thrashing?
OS.short_answer: A state where the system spends more time swapping pages than executing.

OS.question: What is a System Call?
OS.short_answer: A way for programs to interact with the operating system kernel.

OS.question: What is Kernel?
OS.short_answer: The core part of OS that manages system resources and hardware communication.

OS.question: What is Context Switching?
OS.short_answer: Saving state of a process/thread so it can be restored and execution resumed later.

OS.question: What is Semaphores?
OS.short_answer: A variable or abstract data type used to control access to a common resource.

OS.question: Difference between Mutex and Semaphore?
OS.short_answer: Mutex is locking mechanism (one thread); Semaphore is signaling mechanism (multiple).

OS.question: What is Belady's Anomaly?
OS.short_answer: Increasing page frames can sometimes increase page faults (in FIFO).

OS.question: What is Demand Paging?
OS.short_answer: Loading pages into memory only when they are needed.

OS.question: What is Critical Section?
OS.short_answer: A part of a program that accesses a shared resource.

OS.question: What is Round Robin Scheduling?
OS.short_answer: CPU scheduling algorithm where each process gets a small unit of CPU time (time quantum).

OS.question: What is Banker's Algorithm?
OS.short_answer: Algotithm used for deadlock avoidance by checking resource allocation safety.

DBMS.question: What is DBMS?
DBMS.short_answer: Software used to manage databases.

DBMS.question: What is a Database?
DBMS.short_answer: An organized collection of structured information or data.

DBMS.question: What are ACID properties?
DBMS.short_answer: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability.

DBMS.question: What is Normalization?
DBMS.short_answer: Process of organizing data to reduce redundancy and dependency.

DBMS.question: What is 1NF?
DBMS.short_answer: Atomic values; no repeating groups.

DBMS.question: What is 2NF?
DBMS.short_answer: 1NF + no partial dependency.

DBMS.question: What is 3NF?
DBMS.short_answer: 2NF + no transitive dependency.

DBMS.question: What is BCNF?
DBMS.short_answer: A stronger version of 3NF; every determinant is a candidate key.

DBMS.question: What is a Primary Key?
DBMS.short_answer: Uniquely identifies each record in a table.

DBMS.question: What is a Foreign Key?
DBMS.short_answer: A field that refers to the primary key of another table.

DBMS.question: Difference between DELETE and TRUNCATE?
DBMS.short_answer: DELETE is DML (removes rows, can rollback); TRUNCATE is DDL (removes all, faster).

DBMS.question: What is a Join in SQL?
DBMS.short_answer: Clause used to combine rows from two or more tables based on a related column.

DBMS.question: Types of Joins?
DBMS.short_answer: Inner, Left, Right, Full, Cross Self Join.

DBMS.question: What is an Index?
DBMS.short_answer: A data structure that improves the speed of data retrieval operations.

DBMS.question: What is a Transaction?
DBMS.short_answer: A sequence of operations performed as a single logical unit of work.

DBMS.question: Difference between SQL and NoSQL?
DBMS.short_answer: SQL is relational/structured; NoSQL is non-relational/distributed.

DBMS.question: What is Database Sharding?
DBMS.short_answer: Horizontal partitioning of data across multiple databases.

DBMS.question: What is View in SQL?
DBMS.short_answer: A virtual table based on the result-set of an SQL statement.

DBMS.question: What is Stored Procedure?
DBMS.short_answer: Prepared SQL code that you can save and reuse.

DBMS.question: What is Entity-Relationship (ER) Diagram?
DBMS.short_answer: Visual representation of entities and their relationships.

CN.question: What is a Computer Network?
CN.short_answer: A set of computers connected for sharing resources.

CN.question: What is the OSI Model?
CN.short_answer: A framework describing network functions in 7 layers.

CN.question: Name the 7 layers of OSI Model.
CN.short_answer: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application.

CN.question: What is TCP/IP?
CN.short_answer: Suite of communication protocols used to interconnect network devices on the internet.

CN.question: Difference between TCP and UDP?
CN.short_answer: TCP is connection-oriented/reliable; UDP is connectionless/fast.

CN.question: What is an IP Address?
CN.short_answer: Numerical label assigned to each device connected to a network.

CN.question: What is DNS?
CN.short_answer: Domain Name System; translates domain names to IP addresses.

CN.question: What is HTTP vs HTTPS?
CN.short_answer: HTTPS is HTTP with encryption (SSL/TLS).

CN.question: What is a Router?
CN.short_answer: Device that forwards data packets between computer networks.

CN.question: What is an ARP?
CN.short_answer: Address Resolution Protocol; maps IP address to MAC address.

CN.question: What is a MAC Address?
CN.short_answer: Unique identifier assigned to a network interface controller (NIC).

CN.question: What is the Three-way Handshake?
CN.short_answer: Proccess used in TCP to establish a connection (SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK).

CN.question: What is a Subnet?
CN.short_answer: A logical subdivision of an IP network.

CN.question: What is Latency?
CN.short_answer: The time it takes for data to travel from source to destination.

CN.question: What is Bandwidth?
CN.short_answer: The maximum rate of data transfer across a network path.

CN.question: What is a Gateway?
CN.short_answer: A node that serves as an entrance to another network.

CN.question: What is DHCP?
CN.short_answer: Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol; assigns IP addresses dynamically.

CN.question: What is ICMP?
CN.short_answer: Internet Control Message Protocol; used for error reporting (e.g., Ping).

CN.question: What is Firewall?
CN.short_answer: A network security system that monitors and controls traffic.

CN.question: What is VPN?
CN.short_answer: Virtual Private Network; creates a secure connection over public network.

OOP.question: What is OOP?
OOP.short_answer: Programming paradigm based on objects and classes.

OOP.question: Name the 4 pillars of OOP.
OOP.short_answer: Encapsulation, Abstraction, Inheritance, Polymorphism.

OOP.question: What is a Class?
OOP.short_answer: A blueprint or template for creating objects.

OOP.question: What is an Object?
OOP.short_answer: A real-world entity; an instance of a class.

OOP.question: What is Encapsulation?
OOP.short_answer: Wrapping data and methods together; hiding internal state.

OOP.question: What is Abstraction?
OOP.short_answer: Hiding implementation details and showing only functionality.

OOP.question: What is Inheritance?
OOP.short_answer: Mechanism where one class acquires properties of another.

OOP.question: What is Polymorphism?
OOP.short_answer: The ability of an object to take many forms (e.g., overading/overloading).

OOP.question: Compile-time vs Runtime Polymorphism?
OOP.short_answer: Compile-time (Overloading); Runtime (Overriding).

OOP.question: What is Method Overloading?
OOP.short_answer: Multiple methods with same name but different parameters.

OOP.question: What is Method Overriding?
OOP.short_answer: Redefining a base class method in a derived class.

OOP.question: What is an Abstract Class?
OOP.short_answer: A class that cannot be instantiated; may contain abstract methods.

OOP.question: What is an Interface?
OOP.short_answer: A contract that defines methods a class must implement.

OOP.question: Difference between Abstract Class and Interface?
OOP.short_answer: Abstract class can have state/impl; Interface can only have method signatures.

OOP.question: What is a Constructor?
OOP.short_answer: Special method called when an object is instantiated.

OOP.question: What is a Destructor?
OOP.short_answer: Special method called when an object is destroyed.

OOP.question: What is 'this' keyword?
OOP.short_answer: A reference to the current object instance.

OOP.question: What is 'super' or 'base' keyword?
OOP.short_answer: Reference to the parent class object.

OOP.question: What is Access Modifiers?
OOP.short_answer: Keywords used to set visibility of classes/members (public, private, protected).

OOP.question: What is Composition?
OOP.short_answer: Designing classes by grouping objects of other classes ('has-a' relationship).
